evals.prompt: Run a security audit on my upiagent project — I'm about to go live with real merchants
evals.expected_output: Full structured report covering all 10 categories with actual file references, severity ratings, and concrete remediation steps

evals.prompt: I want to add a new API route at /api/payments/refund that lets merchants reverse a verified payment. Can you help?
evals.expected_output: Should flag security considerations (auth, idempotency, amount validation, audit trail) before or alongside implementation

evals.prompt: I'm worried about prompt injection — someone could craft a fake bank email that tricks the LLM into saying a payment was verified when it wasn't. How safe are we?
evals.expected_output: Deep dive into prompt injection category with actual code references to sanitization, schema validation, confidence thresholds

evals.prompt: Can you check if all my API endpoints are properly rate limited? I don't want someone spamming the verify endpoint or brute-forcing API keys
evals.expected_output: Enumerate all endpoints, identify which have rate limiting and which don't, check for bypass vectors like header spoofing, flag in-memory rate limit state issues

evals.prompt: What happens if two verify requests hit at the same time for the same payment? Could both succeed and double-count?
evals.expected_output: Analyze concurrency in verify flow — in-memory dedup, database status transitions, lack of row-level locking, race window between read and write
